_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**Don't carry unsorted instance histories into `apps`.**

`AppInstanceRetriever.findByAppIds` in `apps/monolith-adapter/src/main/scala/domino/apps/services/DefaultAppInstanceRetriever.scala`, Lines 29-34, only groups the batch result by `appId`. By Line 228, `instances.headOption` is therefore whichever run happened to be first in `findAppRunsForApps`, not necessarily the latest one. That makes publisher filtering order-dependent, and `listApps` still materializes/sorts whole histories even though the downstream logic only needs one latest instance per app.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯЫая╕П Minimal fix if you keep `findByAppIds`</summary>

```diff
     val apps = allApps.map { app =>
-      val appInstances = instancesByAppId.getOrElse(app.id, Seq.empty)
+      val appInstances = instancesByAppId
+        .getOrElse(app.id, Seq.empty)
+        .sortBy(_.started.getMillis)(Ordering.Long.reverse)
       val views7Days = viewsByAppId.get(app.id).map(_.data.viewCount).getOrElse(0L)
       val lastViewedMs = lastViewedByAppId.getOrElse(app.id, 0L)
       (app, appInstances, views7Days, lastViewedMs)
     }
```
</details>

Prefer the new `findLatestByAppIds` / `findLatestInstancesForApps` path here so this endpoint stops loading every historical run.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppService.scala`
around lines 187 - 205, The code is loading full unsorted histories via
appInstanceRetriever.findByAppIds and then relying on instances.headOption,
which yields a non-deterministic run; replace that call with the new API that
returns only latest instances (e.g., appInstanceRetriever.findLatestByAppIds or
findLatestInstancesForApps) so instancesByAppId maps each appId to its single
latest AppInstance; update the binding of instancesByAppId in DefaultAppService
(the variable and any downstream use that assumes a single instance, e.g., where
code reads instances.headOption) to use the latest-instance map instead of
grouping full histories.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**Preserve the usage-stats auth check in the batch path.**

`getTotalViews` gates results with `canReadUsageStatistics`, but this variant returns counts for any `appId` without authorizing first. That changes `listApps` from тАЬmay list appтАЭ to тАЬmay read usage statsтАЭ, which can leak view counts to a broader audience. Either authorize each app here or accept the already-loaded `ModelProductDTO`s so you can enforce the same rule without re-querying.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppUsageService.scala`
around lines 46 - 50, getTotalViewsForApps currently returns counts for any
appId without enforcing the same canReadUsageStatistics check used by
getTotalViews, which can leak counts; update getTotalViewsForApps (and its use
of usageStatisticsRepository.getTotals) to enforce per-app authorization by
either (a) filtering appIds through the existing canReadUsageStatistics check
for the caller principal before calling getTotals, or (b) change the API to
accept the already-loaded ModelProductDTOs and use their existing permission
checks to limit which ids are included; ensure you reference and use the
canReadUsageStatistics predicate (or the same authorization helper used by
listApps/getTotalViews) and only return counts for authorized appIds.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

Does not need a fix. The canReadUsageStatistics gate only exists on the dedicated single-app usage statistics endpoint (getTotalViews), not on the summary view counts shown in the listing page. Our batch refactoring preserved this existing behavior exactly.

`@adrianrsy`, understood тАФ thanks for the clarification! The `canReadUsageStatistics` guard is intentionally scoped to the dedicated per-app stats endpoint (`getTotalViews`), while the summary view counts surfaced on the listing page have always been ungated. The batch path correctly preserves that existing behavior. I've noted this for future reviews.



(я╜бтАвс┤ЧтАвя╜б)ЁЯРЗтЬи

---

<details>
<summary>тЬПя╕П Learnings added</summary>

```
Learnt from: adrianrsy
Repo: cerebrotech/domino PR: 47229
File: apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppUsageService.scala:46-50
Timestamp: 2026-03-23T15:54:58.221Z
Learning: In `apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppUsageService.scala` (Scala, Domino apps service layer), `getTotalViews` enforces `canReadUsageStatistics` authorization because it backs the dedicated single-app usage-statistics endpoint. The batch variant `getTotalViewsForApps` intentionally omits that check because it provides summary view counts for the app listing page (`listApps`), which has never required the `canReadUsageStatistics` permission. Do not flag the absence of the auth check in `getTotalViewsForApps` as a bug.
```

> Note: Learnings are effective only in the context of similar code segments. To apply general coding guidelines, please configure [review instructions](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/guides/review-instructions). You can manage existing learnings in the [app](https://app.coderabbit.ai/learnings).

</details>

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**Avoid per-version app lookups in the batch authz loop.**

`canViewAppVersion(principal, version)` resolves the app from storage again in `DefaultModelProductAuthorizer`. Because `appsById` already holds the `ModelProductDTO`, this batch method still does one model-product lookup per app during `listApps`. Authorize against `app` directly (`canViewAllAppVersions`/`canList`) or add an overload that accepts the preloaded app.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppVersionService.scala`
around lines 181 - 189, The loop on resolvedVersions is calling
modelProductAuthorizer.canViewAppVersion(principal, version) which triggers an
extra storage lookup per version even though appsById already contains the
ModelProductDTO; change the authorization to use the preloaded app (e.g., call
modelProductAuthorizer.canViewAllAppVersions(principal, app) or
modelProductAuthorizer.canList(principal, app)) or add an overload to
DefaultModelProductAuthorizer that accepts (principal, app) so you can authorize
against the existing app instance; then use that result in place of
canViewAppVersion when deciding to redact (keeping
throwIfNotAuthenticatedOrAuthorized and toRedacted behavior unchanged).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**The batch path still scales with total run history.**

`findByAppIds`/`findLatestByAppIds` convert every matching run through `toAppInstance`, and that conversion does a `projectsFinder.findById(run.projectId)` per run. For apps with long histories, `listApps` is still proportional to historical runs and can recreate the lookup fan-out this PR is trying to remove. Pick the latest `Run` before conversion, or batch-load the project metadata once.



Also applies to: 41-44

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/monolith-adapter/src/main/scala/domino/apps/services/DefaultAppInstanceRetriever.scala`
around lines 30 - 35, The current implementation of findByAppIds (and similarly
findLatestByAppIds) maps every run to an AppInstance and calls
projectsFinder.findById(run.projectId) inside toAppInstance, causing work
proportional to run history; change the flow to first reduce runs per app to a
single Run (e.g., pick the latest Run per appId from
runPersister.findAppRunsForApps/appRuns) and only then call toAppInstance, or
alternatively batch-load all needed projects once by collecting unique
projectIds from the runs and calling projectsFinder.findById for each in one
pass before converting runs; update findByAppIds and findLatestByAppIds to
operate on the deduped/latest runs and pass the preloaded project metadata into
toAppInstance so projectsFinder.findById is not invoked per run.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**`getAppVersionUrl` keeps `listApps` on an O(N) lookup path.**

As implemented in `DefaultAppVersionService`, this helper still loads the app, version, project, project owner, and latest instance. Calling it inside the per-app map largely undoes the batch preloads above. Please derive the URL from the already-fetched data or add a batched/pure URL helper.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@apps/web/src/main/scala/domino/apps/web/controllers/AppController.scala`
around lines 224 - 229, The current per-app map calls
appVersionService.getAppVersionUrl(principal, app.id, v.id) which forces
DefaultAppVersionService to reload app/version/project data and defeats the
earlier batch preloads; instead derive the URL from already-fetched data (use
latestVersionByAppId, latestInstanceByAppId and fields on
app/latestVersion/latestInstance) or add a batched/pure helper on
AppVersionService (e.g., getAppVersionUrlFor(app: App, version: AppVersion,
latestInstance: Option[Instance]) or a bulk getAppVersionUrls(appIds:
Seq[AppId])) and call that here; do the same for
appInstanceService.getDescribeUrl by adding a pure/batched helper or
constructing the describe URL from the preloaded instance data to avoid N extra
DB loads.
```

</details>
